title: Aerosol delivery system
abstract: An aerosol delivery system has a fluid transfer article with a first region for holding an aerosol precursor and a second region to which the aerosol precursor is transferred from the first region. The second region includes a multiplicity of capillary tubes which extend from the first region to an end of the fluid-transfer article which is at or proximate a heating surface of a heater of the aerosol delivery system. The aerosol precursor passes along the capillary tubes and interacts thermally with the heater to form an aerosol from said aerosol precursor. The first region may be formed from a porous polymer material. An air-flow pathway extends along the heating surface with that air-flow pathway passing the ends of the capillary tubes. When the user sucks or inhales through the aerosol delivery system, air-flows through the air-flow pathway along and the heating surface, so that aerosol passes from the heater and/or the ends of the capillary tubes to the user in the air-flow.
claims: Claims Claims: 1 . An aerosol-generation apparatus comprising a heater and a fluid-transfer article, the fluid- transfer article comprising a first region for holding an aerosol precursor and for transferring said aerosol precursor to a second region of said fluid-transfer article, said second region being formed from a multiplicity of capillary tubes, each of said multiplicity of capillary tubes extending from said first region to an end of said fluid-transfer article facing said heater, said multiplicity of capillary tubes terminating at, or proximate a heating surface of said heater, there being an air-flow pathway along said heating surface, which air-flow pathway passes the ends of said multiplicity of capillary tubes furthest from said first region of said fluid transfer article. 2. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the multiplicity of capillary tubes are generally parallel. 3. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to claim 2, wherein adjacent ones of said multiplicity of capillary tubes contact each other along at least part of their length. 4. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said ends of said multiplicity of capillary tubes furthest from said first region of said fluid-transfer article are tapered. 5. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the multiplicity of capillary tubes form a two-dimensional array at said heating surface. 6. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said multiplicity of capillary tubes are formed of a polymer material. 7. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first region of said fluid-transfer article comprises an empty tank for the receipt and storage of said aerosol precursor. 8. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the first region of said fluid-transfer article is formed from porous polymer material. 9. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to claim 8, wherein said porous polymer material comprises Polyetherimide (PEI). 10. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to claim 8, wherein said porous polymer material comprises Polyether ether ketone (PEEK). 1 1 . An aerosol-generation apparatus according to claim 8, wherein said porous polymer material comprises Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE). 12. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to claim 8, wherein said porous polymer material comprises Polyimide (PI). 13. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to claim 8, wherein said polymeric wicking material comprises Polyethersylphone (PES). 14. An aerosol-generation apparatus article according to claim 8, wherein said porous polymer material comprises Ultra-High Molecular Weight Polyethylene (UHMWPE) 15. An aerosol-generation apparatus according to claim 8, wherein said porous polymer material comprises Polypropylene (PP). 16. An aerosol-generation apparatus article according to claim 8, wherein said porous polymer material comprises Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET). 17. An aerosol delivery system comprising an aerosol-generation apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 16 and a carrier comprising a housing containing said fluid-transfer article. 18. An aerosol-delivery system according to claim 17, further including a further housing containing said heater, said housing and said further housing being separable. 19. An aerosol delivery system according to claim 18, wherein said further housing has an inlet and an outlet, said air-flow pathway extending between said inlet and said outlet.
